findings.summary: kicad_version reported as 'unknown' for file_version 20230121 (KiCad 7); LT3045xMSE (positive LDO) marked inverting=True in main hierarchical sheet; RF chain/signal-path detectors entirely missed f...

correct.description: 11 op-amp circuit instances are detected: ADA4898-2 dual-channel units (buffer gain=1.0 confirmed for U7/U8 unit 1), OPA695 transimpedance/buffer configurations, and U14 correctly identified as inverting with gain=-0.78 (R_in/R_fb from the error_signal sheet). The 'unknown' entries for unit 3 (EP/exposed-pad pseudo-unit) are expected behavior.

R2 (30k resistor) genuinely uses footprint Capacitor_SMD:C_0603_1608Metric — a design-level error faithfully detected. D2/D5/D6 (PMEG2010 Schottky diodes) use SOT-23 package, which doesn't match standard diode footprint filters — also a real and correctly flagged mismatch.

incorrect.description: All four schematics have file_version 20230121, which corresponds to KiCad 7. The analyzer outputs kicad_version='unknown' instead of '7'. The file version to KiCad version mapping appears to be missing this version entry.

In the main hierarchical schematic, U2 (LT3045xMSE) and U3 (LT3045xMSE) are both flagged inverting=True. LT3045 is a positive LDO — it is not an inverting topology. In the power_supply sub-sheet where the same regulators appear, they are correctly reported as inverting=None. The false positive likely arises from parsing the SET-pin resistor network in the hierarchical context. LT3094xMSE (U1, genuine inverting negative LDO) correctly gets inverting=True.

The analyzer flags 'no_driver' ERC warnings on V- (in PLL_VCO and error_signal sub-sheets) and -15V (in power_supply sub-sheet). These rails are legitimately driven from the parent sheet via hierarchical labels or the backplane connector J1. Sub-sheets analyzed in isolation will always show these rails as lacking a driver. The warning fires in both the sub-sheet AND the main sheet for -15V, which is redundant. -15V is supplied by J1's passive connector pins (a30-a32), which is valid for a backplane-powered module.

missed.description: This is a precision 110 MHz molecular transition spectroscopy (MTS) module with an explicit RF signal chain: CVCO45CL VCO, HMC1031 phase-frequency detector, SYPD-1 Mini-Circuits mixer, and 49.9-ohm SMA-connected RF paths. rf_chains=0 and rf_matching=0 across all sheets. The 49.9-ohm termination resistors are being classified as RC high-pass filters instead. None of the RF ICs (VCO, PFD, mixer) are recognized as RF chain elements.

findings.summary: RC filter detection finds PLL loop filter components correctly

correct.description: R7 (140 ohm) + C18 (330n) low-pass at 3.4 kHz and R8 (287 ohm) + C17 (22n) low-pass at 25 kHz are reasonable PLL loop filter poles. R3/R10/R16 (49.9 ohm) + capacitor high-pass filters are identified, though in practice these are RF terminations — the filter classification is debatable but not clearly wrong.

findings.summary: Power regulator chain correctly traced: +15V → LM7812 → +12V → LM7808 → +8V → LT3045 → +5V/+3V, plus negative rail; PWR_FLAG warning on GND fired despite PWR_FLAG symbols present in the design

correct.description: The six-regulator power chain is fully and correctly identified: U10 (LM7812, +15V→+12V), U12 (LM7808, +12V→+8V), U11 (LM7908, -15V→-8V), U1 (LT3094, -8V→-5V), U2 (LT3045, +8V→+5V), U3 (LT3045, +8V→+3V). Rail names and topologies are accurate in the sub-sheet context.

incorrect.description: The power_supply sheet lists 'PWR_FLAG' in its power_rails, yet pwr_flag_warnings still fires for GND. The sub-sheet analysis appears to be checking GND coverage independently without resolving that the PWR_FLAG in the sheet satisfies the ERC requirement. This is a false positive.

findings.summary: 35 courtyard overlap false positives from J6 RF shield can footprint; PCB routing correctly reported as complete (119 nets, 0 unrouted, routing_complete=True); DFM board size warning correctly flag...

correct.description: The 'routed_nets=61' vs 'total_nets_with_pads=119' reflects that 58 nets are 'unconnected-' prefixed no-connect nets (intentional, from J1 DIN41612 backplane connector unused pins). True signal nets = 61, all routed. The routing_complete=True and unrouted=0 are accurate.
Board is 160x100mm, which correctly exceeds the JLCPCB 100x100mm standard-tier threshold and warrants a pricing tier warning. DFM metrics (0.2mm track, 0.3mm drill) are standard tier.

incorrect.description: J6 is a Laird BMI-S-107 one-piece RF shield can (44.37x44.37mm). Its courtyard intentionally encloses the components it shields. The analyzer flags 35 overlaps between J6 and interior components (U15, J4, U7, U16, etc.) as placement violations. These are expected for a shield-can footprint and should be suppressed. The 11 non-J6/non-J1 overlaps (e.g., D2/U6, J11/U11) may be genuine concerns worth reporting.

J1 is a DIN41612 C-type 2x32 horizontal through-hole backplane connector. Its courtyard extends 76mm below the board edge (y=148.8 board bottom, courtyard to y=224.97) because the mating housing protrudes below the PCB edge. This is by design — the component is a card-edge connector. The -76mm edge clearance warning is a false positive for this connector type.
